methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createFromJson
methods.description: Creates a StockMaterial object from given JSON string.

methods.name: toJson
methods.description: Generates and returns a JSON string that contains a description of this StockMaterial.

properties.name: category
properties.description: Gets and sets the category of the stock material.

properties.name: designators
properties.description: Gets a list of designators of the stock material.

properties.name: hardness
properties.description: Get and sets the hardness of the stock materials. NOTE: the hardness can be NaN if not set. and user can set the hardness to NaN.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Gets and sets the name of the stock material.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

category.title: StockMaterial.category Property
category.description: Gets and sets the category of the stock material.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = stockMaterial_var.category

# Set the value of the property.
stockMaterial_var.category = propertyValue

designators.title: StockMaterial.designators Property
designators.description: Gets a list of designators of the stock material.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = stockMaterial_var.designators

# Set the value of the property.
stockMaterial_var.designators = propertyValue

hardness.title: StockMaterial.hardness Property
hardness.description: Get and sets the hardness of the stock materials. NOTE: the hardness can be NaN if not set. and user can set the hardness to NaN.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = stockMaterial_var.hardness

# Set the value of the property.
stockMaterial_var.hardness = propertyValue

isValid.title: StockMaterial.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = stockMaterial_var.isValid

name.title: StockMaterial.name Property
name.description: Gets and sets the name of the stock material.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = stockMaterial_var.name

# Set the value of the property.
stockMaterial_var.name = propertyValue

objectType.title: StockMaterial.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = stockMaterial_var.objectType

classType.title: StockMaterial.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createFromJson.title: StockMaterial.createFromJson Method
createFromJson.description: Creates a StockMaterial object from given JSON string.

parameters.name: json
parameters.description: The JSON should fully define the StockMaterial and contain all StockMaterial parameters. If the JSON contains more than one StockMaterial, only the first StockMaterial is loaded.

toJson.title: StockMaterial.toJson Method
toJson.description: Generates and returns a JSON string that contains a description of this StockMaterial.
toJson.return_description: Returns a JSON string that contains a description of this StockMaterial.
